Package org.solenopsis.soap.metadata

Class MessagingChannel

java.lang.Object
org.solenopsis.soap.metadata.Metadata
org.solenopsis.soap.metadata.MessagingChannel
public class MessagingChannel extends Metadata

Java class for MessagingChannel complex type

.

The following schema fragment specifies the expected content contained within this class.


 <complexType name="MessagingChannel">
   <complexContent>
     <extension base="{http://soap.sforce.com/2006/04/metadata}Metadata">
       <sequence>
         <element name="automatedResponses" type="{http://soap.sforce.com/2006/04/metadata}MessagingAutoResponse" maxOccurs="unbounded" minOccurs="0"/>
         <element name="customParameters" type="{http://soap.sforce.com/2006/04/metadata}MessagingChannelCustomParameter" maxOccurs="unbounded" minOccurs="0"/>
         <element name="description" type="{http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema}string" minOccurs="0"/>
         <element name="masterLabel" type="{http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema}string"/>
         <element name="messagingChannelType" type="{http://soap.sforce.com/2006/04/metadata}MessagingChannelType"/>
         <element name="sessionHandlerFlow" type="{http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema}string" minOccurs="0"/>
         <element name="sessionHandlerQueue" type="{http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema}string"/>
         <element name="sessionHandlerType" type="{http://soap.sforce.com/2006/04/metadata}MessagingSessionHandlerType"/>
         <element name="standardParameters" type="{http://soap.sforce.com/2006/04/metadata}MessagingChannelStandardParameter" maxOccurs="unbounded" minOccurs="0"/>
       </sequence>
     </extension>
   </complexContent>
 </complexType>

  • Method Details

    • getAutomatedResponses

      public List<MessagingAutoResponse> getAutomatedResponses()
      Gets the value of the automatedResponses property.

      This accessor method returns a reference to the live list, not a snapshot. Therefore, any modification you make to the returned list will be present inside the Jakarta XML Binding object. This is why there is not a set method for the automatedResponses property.

      For example, to add a new item, do as follows: 

       getAutomatedResponses().add(newItem);

      Objects of the following type(s) are allowed in the list MessagingAutoResponse

      Returns:
      The value of the automatedResponses property.

    • getCustomParameters

      public List<MessagingChannelCustomParameter> getCustomParameters()
      Gets the value of the customParameters property.

      This accessor method returns a reference to the live list, not a snapshot. Therefore, any modification you make to the returned list will be present inside the Jakarta XML Binding object. This is why there is not a set method for the customParameters property.

      For example, to add a new item, do as follows: 

       getCustomParameters().add(newItem);

      Objects of the following type(s) are allowed in the list MessagingChannelCustomParameter

      Returns:
      The value of the customParameters property.

    • getStandardParameters

      public List<MessagingChannelStandardParameter> getStandardParameters()
      Gets the value of the standardParameters property.

      This accessor method returns a reference to the live list, not a snapshot. Therefore, any modification you make to the returned list will be present inside the Jakarta XML Binding object. This is why there is not a set method for the standardParameters property.

      For example, to add a new item, do as follows: 

       getStandardParameters().add(newItem);

      Objects of the following type(s) are allowed in the list MessagingChannelStandardParameter

      Returns:
      The value of the standardParameters property.
